actionable_feedback: Ensure the workbook is programmatically parseable by standard libraries (openpyxl/pandas). Save as a standard .xlsx (no proprietary container) and confirm file opens cleanly in a neutral environment.

Include a workbook tab (or separate CSV) that exports formula text for every derived cell (use Excel FORMULATEXT or a 'Formulas' sheet listing cell addresses and formulas). This allows automated auditors to verify derivations without needing a GUI screenshot.

Add explicit per-record provenance and confidence columns in the Inputs sheet (one citation column per metric or a single structured provenance column) linking each metric to the source packet line(s). Update the review doc to map those provenance entries to the packet.

Add a short verification script (Python notebook or script) that recomputes derived columns from the Inputs sheet and asserts equality with the Calc sheet values; include instructions to run it. This will remove the need for manual formula inspection in constrained grader environments.

If library restrictions persist, include a CSV export of the workbook (Inputs and Calc) plus a separate CSV containing formula text or a screenshot of a representative formula; but prefer the formula-text export first.

In claims_review.md, add explicit sheet-and-cell references (e.g., 'Calc!C10 = A2/A$10') for the main derived metrics and mention the location of the formula-export and verification script so graders can validate without opening the GUI.
Re-run the sensitivity analysis after adding provenance/confidence columns (if those columns influence numeric decisions) and document any change in numeric gate outcomes and board recommendations.
artifact_specific_comments: claims_review.md: well-documented; includes governing values, methods, formulas, recommendation table, and sensitivity checks-meets rubric expectations.

claims_substantiation_table.xlsx: present but could not be parsed by the grader environment ('No module named "openpyxl"'), so automated verification of formula presence and per-record citations inside the workbook could not be completed.
feedback_summary: Deliverables are high quality: claims_review.md documents governing values, methods, formulas, recommendations, and sensitivity checks. The only material verification gap is inability to programmatically parse/inspect the .xlsx (openpyxl unavailable in the grader environment). That prevented automated confirmation that every derived cell contains auditable formulas and that per-record provenance/confidence citations are present inside the workbook. Near-complete credit awarded, but fix required to remove reproducibility/verification friction.

acceptance_criteria_for_revised_submissi: The .xlsx opens with openpyxl/pandas without errors in a neutral environment
A 'Formulas' sheet or CSV includes formula text for all derived cells (or a representative export plus explanation if large)
Per-record provenance and confidence columns are present and reference packet lines for each metric
Verification script runs and validates that derived values match formulas; results included in claims_review.md (pass/fail and brief output)
Claims review still documents governing values, methods, sensitivity analysis, and updated board recommendation if provenance/confidence changes the outcome
extra_model_fields.notes: Priority is to remove verification friction by exposing formulas and providing an automated check. The substantive analytic approach and recommendations in claims_review.md appear sound and should be preserved.
suggested_attachment_list_for_revision: claims_substantiation_table_formulas.csv or workbook tab 'Formulas' with FORMULATEXT output
verification_script.py or verification_notebook.ipynb that recomputes derived scores from Inputs and asserts equality
updated claims_review.md with explicit sheet/cell references and instructions to run verification script
optional: screenshot.png of a representative formula if formula export is impossible
